description: Replace the mock/simulated model download in AIModelRepositoryImpl with a real HTTP download from Hugging Face using OkHttp. Add OkHttp dependency, INTERNET permission, and implement streaming download with progress reporting.
steps: Add INTERNET and ACCESS_NETWORK_STATE permissions to app/src/main/AndroidManifest.xml before the <application> tag

Rewrite the downloadModel() function in app/src/main/java/com/localai/data/repository/AIModelRepositoryImpl.kt to: (a) construct the Hugging Face download URL as https://huggingface.co/{modelInfo.id}/resolve/main/{modelInfo.ggufFileName}, (b) use OkHttp to make a GET request, (c) stream the response body to a temporary file (e.g. targetFile.gguf.tmp) with progress emissions based on bytes read vs Content-Length, (d) rename the tmp file to the final targetFile on success, (e) delete the tmp file on failure, (f) emit DownloadState.Failed on any exception (IOException, etc.)
Keep the existing early-return guard: if (targetFile.exists() && targetFile.length() > 4096) emit Completed and return

Inject OkHttpClient via Hilt constructor injection into AIModelRepositoryImpl, or create it locally. If injecting via Hilt, add an @Provides function in a new or existing Hilt module (e.g. app/src/main/java/com/localai/di/NetworkModule.kt) that provides a singleton OkHttpClient with appropriate timeouts for large file downloads (e.g. 30s connect, 5min read/write)
Ensure the Flow uses Dispatchers.IO for the network/disk operations (use flowOn(Dispatchers.IO) at the end of the flow builder)
Remove the old 'delay(300)' simulation loop and the 'import kotlinx.coroutines.delay' if no longer needed
acceptance_criteria: AIModelRepositoryImpl.downloadModel() makes a real HTTP GET request to Hugging Face URL
Download streams to a .tmp file first, then renames on success
Progress is reported as bytes_downloaded / content_length
Errors are caught and emitted as DownloadState.Failed with a descriptive message
The existing early-return guard for already-downloaded files is preserved
Code compiles without errors (verified by Gradle build or syntax check)
verification: Run ./gradlew assembleDebug (or if Android SDK not available, verify Kotlin syntax correctness of modified files)
